mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of low inflation reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative or flat 5-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate inflation hedging and rate hike pricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation months over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining real personal consumption expenditures signals a demand contraction that outweighs supply-side resilience, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative co-movement in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment during a monetary tightening cycle indicates a supply-demand mismatch that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 50% of occurrences during tightening periods.
